duration: Total of 5 to 8 minutes. Up to one minute continuous viewing.
specific_location: Front, south-facing side of Regent Administrative Center on the campus of the University of Colorado Boulder near the intersection of Broadway and Regent Drive.
description: Flycatcher: bright greenish yellow; yellowish throughout the chest as well as head and back with head and back more of a darkish yellow-green; round head; small bill (with darker upper mandible); bright white wing-bars on black wings with top one one-half the length of the bottom one; wings showing white edges on the secondaries; round buffy-white eye-ring; white outside edges on the tail, seemingly short primary projections.Behaviors: The bird was first noticed outside my office window flycatching in a barren honey-locust tree. It would perch very still and the swoop out and return to another perch. This was like a wood-pewee, but lighter and more fluttery. The bird was very close to my window and flew out of view after a couple of forrays. When I had the opportunity to go outside, I stepped onto a balcony at the top of a second story staircase, and was amazed to see the bird perched right in front of me on the rail. It remained in this position for 30 to 60 seconds (actually it was checking me out just as I was it!) Finally it flew down to the ground and then off.Call: NAPlumage: adult
